page.title: Beginner's Guide
page.description: Step-by-step roadmap from zero to endgame. Quinfall's character cap is level 100; main story quests end at 50. After 50, the Town Board and party-based mob grind become the core XP loop.

hero.title: From Lvl 1 to Lvl 100
hero.body: Quinfall progression is two-tier: levels 1-50 ride the main story. At level 50 the quest stream stops and the game switches to "set your own path". From 50 to 100 the daily Town Board quests and group-based elite-mob hunts become the primary XP source.

quickStart.title: Ten Steps Out the Gate
quickStart.subtitle: Everything you should check before you spawn into the world. The decisions here are the ones that are hardest to undo later.

install.title: Install on Steam
install.body: Grab Quinfall on Steam. About 55 GB, SSD recommended.

server.title: Pick a server
server.body: EU / NA / Asia. EU is the home for Turkish players; ping and population are the best fit.

channel.title: Channel decision
channel.body: PvE channel is safe at the start; PvP channel opens caravan, pirate and world-boss combat.

character.title: Create your character
character.body: Looks can't be changed later. Face, bone, muscle, hair, makeup — every parameter is one-shot. Don't rush.

tutorial.title: Don't skip the tutorial
tutorial.body: The tutorial teaches combat, map, inventory, life skills and farm mechanics. Skipping it makes catching up much harder later.

weapon.title: Pick your first weapon
weapon.body: There are no classes — the weapon is the class. Try multiple weapons in the first 10 levels; commit to a main.

mount.title: Lvl 25-30: first horse
mount.body: Get the first mount on time; lasso a green/blue wild horse. Pabas is the heart of horse breeding.

profession.title: Lvl 35-40: profession
profession.body: Start at least one gathering profession. Endgame gear is hard without a lifeskill economy.

guild.title: Join a guild
guild.body: Without a guild at mid-level you miss sieges, clan island, shared investment. The social layer is the heart of the game.

grade.title: Lvl 50 → 100: board + party
grade.body: At level 50 the main story ends. Past that: daily Town Board quests + party-based elite-mob grind. Solo yield drops fast — make friends.

phases.title: From Lvl 1 to Lvl 100
phases.subtitle: Quinfall progression splits cleanly into five phases. The first four go quick; the fifth phase is the real game — months, not days, are spent here.

tutorial.title: Tutorial and Mechanic Onboarding
tutorial.body: The main story auto-triggers; the tutorial covers combat, map, inventory, life skills and farm basics. This is where you learn the language of the game.
bullets: From the starter city the Old Sage starts the main NPC chain.
Pick up your first weapon — it sets your class.
Low-tier starter gear + first skill points unlock.
If you want, test different weapons through level 10.

build.title: Weapon Family and Stat Allocation
build.body: Your weapon family must commit here. 2 stat points per level; total 200 by level 100. Town Board dailies are the passive XP gate.
bullets: Equip a second weapon; the dual-weapon system unlocks.
Stat allocation: bad investment hurts — respec is limited.
Pick up the first skill books from city merchants.
Get every gear piece to at least 1 star.

dungeon.title: Foaming Catacombs and Lifeskills
dungeon.body: First dungeon opens: Foaming Catacombs. Farm Easy/Medium; start the lifeskill tree at the same time. 1,000 dungeon points refill daily.
bullets: First dungeon: Foaming Catacombs. Ideal for learning mechanics.
Pick a profession: gathering (Harvester / Lumberjack) or crafting (Blacksmith / Alchemy).
A horse is a must — lasso a green/blue near Pabas.
Don't skip Town Board quests — 2x XP gate.

guild.title: Hard Dungeons, Purple Drops, Guild
guild.body: Hard mode dungeons open the purple drop pool. At this point joining a guild becomes practically required — sieges, clan island, shared treasury are now in play.
bullets: Join a guild: siege participation is members-only.
Clan altar in a red zone grants +30% permanent XP buff.
Hard mode dungeons drop purple / top-tier crafting mats.
Caravan guarding pays — work with a guild escort.

endgame.title: Lvl 50 → 100: Board and Party Hunting
endgame.body: At level 50 the main story ends — no new quests. XP comes from two channels: daily Town Board quests and party-based elite-mob hunts. Solo yield drops fast; without a real party you stall after 75.
bullets: Town Board: daily board quests are the main XP gate.
Party mob hunts: take down elite and 1/2/3-star champion mobs as a group.
Stack the Statue +40% XP buff before every session.
Lvl 75-90 slows; Lvl 90-100 needs a group zone to progress.

stats.title: 6 Stats, 2 Weapons, 90 Mastery
stats.subtitle: Every level grants 2 stat points; you spend 200 in total by Lvl 100. You carry two weapons, each with its own 90-level mastery tree.

perLevel.title: 2 points per level
perLevel.body: Every level grants 2 stat points to spend. Plan early; the right build needs the first levels mapped out.

totalAtCap.title: Lvl 100 = 200 points
totalAtCap.body: By the time you cap at Lvl 100 you've spent 200 stat points; where they go defines your build.

mastery90.title: Weapon Mastery 90
mastery90.body: Each weapon earns its own XP and levels up to 90. Each mastery level grants a skill point for that specific weapon.

sixStats.title: 6 core stats
sixStats.body: STR / DEF / CON / INT / RES / MEN. Your build profile is built from these six.

twoWeapons.title: 2 active weapon slots
twoWeapons.body: Carry two weapons on your back, swap mid-fight. Think of it as two classes at once.

noClassLock.title: No class lock
noClassLock.body: Characters don't pick a class — switch the weapon, switch the class. Learn as many weapon families as you want.

respec.title: Respec warning
respec.body: Stat respec is limited and expensive. Trial-and-error is a disaster — research your build, lock the plan in the first 30 levels.

post50.title: When the Story Ends, the Board Begins
post50.subtitle: At Lvl 50 the main story stops carrying you. No new quests. XP comes from two channels: daily Town Board quests and party-based elite-mob hunts. Stack the buff economy and daily progress speeds up.

questEnd.title: Lvl 50 = end of main story
questEnd.body: Community-confirmed: the main story closes at lvl 50. Between 50 and 100 the game offers no new quests; XP comes from two main channels — Town Board dailies and party-based elite-mob hunts. This is exactly where solo players hit the wall.

level50to75.body: A relatively fast band. Steady progress through Town Board quests + duo/trio elite-mob farm. Faster with the Statue buff active.

level75to90.body: Step up to Lvl 75 group zones. Solo yield drops sharply; a 4-5 person party for elite-mob hunting becomes mandatory.

level90to100.body: The slowest band. Lvl 100 group zones + the full buff stack are required. Without Statue + guild + Town Board, daily progress is invisible.

townBoard.title: Town Board
townBoard.body: Town People quests give 2x XP versus Royal Quests. The daily starting point.

statueBuff.title: Statue Buff
statueBuff.body: Buy a 1-hour +40% XP buff at city statues (~2.25M gold). 4h/7h/10h variants exist too.

guildAltar.title: Clan Altar
guildAltar.body: A clan altar in a red zone grants a permanent +30% XP buff. Altar location matters when you pick a guild.

guildBuff.title: Guild Buff
guildBuff.body: A +40% XP buff cycle activated every 12 hours. Time it to your online hours.

townBoard.title: Town Board quests
townBoard.body: Town Board dailies are the spine of post-50 play. 2x XP versus Royal Quests, steady income.

partyMobs.title: Party mob hunting
partyMobs.body: Take down elite and 1/2/3-star champion mobs with friends in your group. Yield is much higher than solo.

highDungeons.title: High-tier dungeons
highDungeons.body: Lvl 75-100 group dungeons are an extra XP channel. Burning the daily 1,000 dungeon points is mandatory.

championStars.title: Champion-star mobs
championStars.body: Starred champion mobs give multiples of standard XP and drops. Worth tracking down.

daily.title: Six Things to Open Every Session
daily.subtitle: The hidden rule of endgame: nail the daily routine. Skip it and you can't catch the players who don't.

townBoard.title: Town Board quests
townBoard.body: Town People quests give 2x XP versus Royal. The first stop of the day.

dungeonPoints.title: Dungeon Points (1,000)
dungeonPoints.body: Refills hourly up to 1,000. Every entry spends from this pool — don't log off without burning them.

quinfallsTower.title: Quinfall's Tower
quinfallsTower.body: Floors that climb in difficulty. An endgame XP and special-reward source.

zenithConquest.title: Zenith Conquest
zenithConquest.body: The main event: PvE / PvP swap daily; 25 per flag, 100 per battlefield.

worldBoss.title: World boss rotation
worldBoss.body: Specific bosses descend every day. Kraken three times a day — line up the guild calendar.

statue.title: Statue buff pickup
statue.body: Buy +40% XP buffs at city statues; stack before activity.

mistakes.title: Avoid Ten Mistakes, Multiply Your Pace
mistakes.subtitle: The ten traps the community calls out the most. Knowing them avoids them — fixing them later costs weeks.

skipTutorial.title: Skipping the tutorial
skipTutorial.body: The tutorial gives combat, life skills and farm mechanics in one pass. Skipping it doubles the time it takes to fill the gap later.

multiWeapon.title: Spreading across weapons
multiWeapon.body: Mastery 30 on six weapons is much weaker than mastery 90 on one. Main first, sub second.

respecWait.title: Banking on respec
respecWait.body: Respec is limited and expensive. Research your build; lock the plan in the first 30 levels.

lowTierEnhance.title: Stars on low-tier gear
lowTierEnhance.body: Pushing a level-30 weapon to 5 stars is wasted — it resets when you swap to higher tier. Save stars for endgame gear.

pvpEarly.title: Switching to PvP channel early
pvpEarly.body: Low-gear in a PvP channel = quick deaths and gear loss. Build up on PvE first.

noGuild.title: Playing without a guild
noGuild.body: Sieges, clan island, guild buffs, treasury — all of it is guild-bound. Solo play misses half the content.

ignoreTownBoard.title: Ignoring Town Board
ignoreTownBoard.body: Town People quests are 2x XP — the main XP gate post-50. Skipping them stacks lost hours at endgame.

noProfession.title: Picking zero professions
noProfession.body: Without a profession you stay outside the economy. At least one gathering profession is mandatory.

cashShopAddict.title: Leaning on the cash shop
cashShopAddict.body: V-Coin isn't pay-to-win; it gives no stat advantage. Gold buys everything — you don't need to spend.

rushed.title: Rushing the climb
rushed.body: Hitting Lvl 50 in a week and asking "what now?" is the classic mistake. Mastery + profession + social network advance in parallel.

timeline.title: Roadmap at a Glance
timeline.subtitle: From hour zero to the endgame threshold, where you should be at each hour. Answers "what should I do today?" at the end of every session.

focus.tutorial: Tutorial, first weapon pick, mechanic learning
focus.sideQuest: Side quests, Town Board, first profession, first skill book
focus.firstDungeon: First dungeon run, first mount, profession grind
focus.guildJoin: Join a guild, hard-mode dungeons, gear at 3 stars
focus.endgameDungeon: Endgame dungeon, first world boss, gear at 4 stars
focus.siege: Town Board + party mob hunts, sieges, gear at 5 stars
